questions.question: Q1. What is the main objective of load balancing in a smart grid?

answers.d: To reduce the number of consumers

explanations.a: Incorrect. Load balancing is not related to pricing.
explanations.b: Correct. Load balancing distributes power demand efficiently across the grid.
explanations.c: Incorrect. It does not stop generation.

questions.question: Q2. Which technology enables real-time monitoring in smart grids?

explanations.a: Correct. Smart meters provide real-time energy usage data.

questions.question: Q3. What indicates an overloaded smart grid?

explanations.c: Correct. Overload occurs when demand exceeds available supply.

questions.question: Q4. Which sector is commonly included in smart grid energy distribution simulations?

explanations.d: Correct. All these sectors are part of energy distribution systems.

questions.question: Q5. What is the benefit of integrating renewable energy sources into a smart grid?

explanations.c: Correct. Renewable sources provide cleaner energy.
explanations.d: They do not intentionally increase losses.

questions.question: Q6. Which parameter is used to determine total energy demand?

explanations.a: Correct. Total demand is the sum of demands from all sectors.

questions.question: Q7. What is the role of a power plant in a smart grid?

explanations.b: Correct. Power plants are responsible for electricity generation.
explanations.d: Household appliances are not controlled by power plants.

questions.question: Q8. What happens when smart grid efficiency increases?

explanations.a: Correct. Higher efficiency reduces energy losses.

questions.question: Q9. Which device helps consumers monitor their electricity consumption?

explanations.c: Correct. Smart meters monitor and report electricity usage.

questions.question: Q10. What is the expected outcome of an effective smart grid energy distribution system?

explanations.a: Smart grids aim to reduce losses.
explanations.b: Correct. Smart grids provide efficient and reliable electricity distribution.
